feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: 7 failing sub-attributes (46.0 pts recoverable), 2 passing. 1 regressions detected — recovery is top priority.

==================================================
REGRESSIONS (RECOVER THESE FIRST — highest priority):
==================================================

  REGRESSED: arg_counter_quality — was 75% at iter 1, now 62% (-12%)
    What was lost: The structural barriers argument was cut off mid-sentence in the current version. The best version contained a complete structural barriers section that explained 'AI regulation suffers from horizontal misalignment between international competitiveness and trustworthy AI, where seeking to win the geopolitical AI race generates proposals to accelerate development that can be in tension with safeguards designed...' This section was providing the full structural argument that earned the higher score.
    Why it dropped: The current version appears to be truncated, ending abruptly with 'While benchmark saturation isn't inherentl' - cutting off both the benchmark saturation argument mid-sentence and completely removing the structural barriers section. This truncation reduced the structural_barriers score from 1.0 to 0.5 because the argument went from 'specific structural argument' to just 'mentioned' due to incompleteness.
    → RECOVER: Complete the truncated benchmark saturation paragraph that ends with 'While benchmark saturation isn't inherentl' and restore the full structural barriers section from the best version, including the complete explanation of regulatory misalignment between competitiveness and safety concerns. Keep all the improved steelman content with specific researcher quotes while ensuring all three counter-arguments are fully developed.

DO NOT CHANGE (these sections are passing):
  ✓ arg_steelman: 75% — keep unchanged
  ✓ arg_readability: 98% — keep unchanged
